Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: On the effect of boron on grain boundary character in a new polycrystalline superalloy

Process_Text.original: Conventional casting (CC) using ceramic moulds with cobalt aluminate nucleation catalyst; moulds de-waxed in steam furnace for 5 min, sintered at 1000C for 1 h prior to casting; casting performed under vacuum with mould temperature approximately 1000C; followed by Hot Isostatic Pressing (HIP) at 1195C for 5 h under 175 MPa pressure; primary ageing at 1120C for 4 h (air cooling); secondary ageing at 845C for 24 h (air cooling)

Process_Info.Equipment: Doncasters Precision Castings Ltd. (ceramic moulds); JEOL 6500F FEGSEM; JEOL 2000FX TEM; Cameca LEAP 3000X HR APT (laser mode, 200 kHz, 0.4 nJ); CAMECA NanoSIMS 50

Microstructure_Text.original: Gamma matrix with gamma-prime precipitates; grain boundary M23C6 Cr-rich carbides (lowest Z contrast in BSE); Ta-rich MC carbides at grain boundaries with elongated/blocky morphology; intragranular MC carbides with script-like shapes; gamma-prime particles decorating grain boundaries; no intergranular M5B3 borides; gamma denuded zones observed
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC γ matrix with γ-prime; grain boundary M23C6 carbides and Ta-rich MC carbides; no borides present

Composition_Info.Note: B = 0.03 at.% (threshold level); balance is Ni

Process_Text.original: Conventional casting (CC) using ceramic moulds with cobalt aluminate nucleation catalyst; moulds de-waxed in steam furnace for 5 min, sintered at 1000C for 1 h prior to casting; casting performed under vacuum with mould temperature approximately 1000C; followed by Hot Isostatic Pressing (HIP) at 1195C for 5 h under 175 MPa pressure; primary ageing at 1120C for 4 h (air cooling); secondary ageing at 845C for 24 h (air cooling)

Process_Info.Equipment: Doncasters Precision Castings Ltd. (ceramic moulds); JEOL 6500F FEGSEM; JEOL 2000FX TEM; Cameca LEAP 3000X HR APT (laser mode, 200 kHz, 0.4 nJ); CAMECA NanoSIMS 50

Microstructure_Text.original: Gamma matrix with gamma-prime precipitates; grain boundary M5B3 Cr-rich borides (lowest Z contrast in BSE); boride particles up to 500 nm length along grain boundaries; Ta-rich MC carbides at grain boundaries; gamma particles from MC carbide breakdown; no M23C6 at grain boundaries; nano-sized coherent M23C6 carbides near grain boundaries in matrix; serrated grain boundary character observed
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC γ matrix with γ-prime; grain boundary M5B3 borides (no M23C6); serrated GB character

Precipitates.Phase_Type: M23C6 Carbide (nano, coherent, in matrix near GB)
Gamma Prime (grain boundary, from MC breakdown)

Composition_Info.Note: B = 0.05 at.% (OPTIMUM boron content per paper findings); balance is Ni

Process_Text.original: Conventional casting (CC) using ceramic moulds with cobalt aluminate nucleation catalyst; moulds de-waxed in steam furnace for 5 min, sintered at 1000C for 1 h prior to casting; casting performed under vacuum with mould temperature approximately 1000C; followed by Hot Isostatic Pressing (HIP) at 1195C for 5 h under 175 MPa pressure; primary ageing at 1120C for 4 h (air cooling); secondary ageing at 845C for 24 h (air cooling)

Process_Info.Equipment: Doncasters Precision Castings Ltd. (ceramic moulds); JEOL 6500F FEGSEM; JEOL 2000FX TEM; Cameca LEAP 3000X HR APT (laser mode, 200 kHz, 0.4 nJ); CAMECA NanoSIMS 50

Microstructure_Text.original: Gamma matrix with gamma-prime precipitates; highest volume fraction of M5B3 Cr-rich borides along grain boundaries (lowest Z contrast); boride particles distributed fairly uniformly up to 500 nm length along GB; serrated grain boundary character; MC carbides present; gamma particles from MC breakdown; no M23C6 carbides at grain boundaries; continuous voids along grain boundaries eliminated due to serrated GB; mixed inter- and transgranular creep failure
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC γ matrix with γ-prime; highest M5B3 boride volume fraction; serrated GB character; best creep performance

Precipitates.Phase_Type: M23C6 Carbide (nano, coherent, in matrix near GB)
Gamma Prime (grain boundary, from MC breakdown)

Composition_Info.Note: B = 0.08 at.% (excessive boron - adverse effect on creep); balance is Ni

Process_Text.original: Conventional casting (CC) using ceramic moulds with cobalt aluminate nucleation catalyst; moulds de-waxed in steam furnace for 5 min, sintered at 1000C for 1 h prior to casting; casting performed under vacuum with mould temperature approximately 1000C; followed by Hot Isostatic Pressing (HIP) at 1195C for 5 h under 175 MPa pressure; primary ageing at 1120C for 4 h (air cooling); secondary ageing at 845C for 24 h (air cooling)

Process_Info.Equipment: Doncasters Precision Castings Ltd. (ceramic moulds); JEOL 6500F FEGSEM; JEOL 2000FX TEM; Cameca LEAP 3000X HR APT (laser mode, 200 kHz, 0.4 nJ); CAMECA NanoSIMS 50

Microstructure_Text.original: Gamma matrix with gamma-prime precipitates; high volume fraction of M5B3 Cr-rich borides along grain boundaries; near-continuous gamma-prime layers along grain boundaries due to MC carbide breakdown; excessive boride precipitation beyond critical volume fraction; serrated grain boundary character; plate-shaped coherent M23C6 carbides near GBs (up to 200 nm length, 30 nm thickness)
Microstructure_Text.simplified: FCC γ matrix with γ-prime; excessive M5B3 borides; near-continuous gamma layers; excessive borides adversely affect creep

Precipitates.Phase_Type: M23C6 Carbide (nano, coherent, near GB in matrix)
